lines.en_lit: who does not avenge and bear a grudge like a snake is not a Torah scholar But isn’t it written “You shall not take vengeance nor bear any grudge That is written with regard to monetary as it was taught What is revenge and what is bearing a grudge? Revenge One said to Lend me your sickle, he said: No. The next day he, said to Lend me your ax. said to him: I will not lend to you, just as you did not lend to me, that is revenge.

And what is bearing a grudge? one said to Lend me your ax, he said: No, the next day he, said to Lend me your robe; said to him: Here it is, I am not like you, who would not lend to me, that is bearing a grudge.

But not personal anguish Wasn’t taught Those who are insulted but do not insult who hear themselves shamed but do not respond, who act out of love and who happy in suffering, about them the verse states: “They that love Him be as the sun when it goes forth in its might”
actually, keep in his heart, But didn’t Rava say: whoever forgoes his reckonings forgoes for all his sins? has sought to appease be appeased
And what do they extend one or two Now two necessary one

Rav Ḥisda said: not difficult. Here, a healthy person There, a sick person, And it was taught They extend one they not extend two. In what is this statement said? in a healthy person; however, a sick person extend even two And alone, extend two but counted only as one.

And counted as only one? Wasn’t it taught not extend the third or the thumb, due to cheaters. But if he does extend the third it is counted for him. thumb, it is not counted for him, and moreover lashes the person in charge of the pakia.
What it is counted for him? also one.
What a pakia? Rav said: a madra. What is a madra? Rav Pappa said: It is a whip [matraka] the Arabs, the end of which is split

Abaye said: At first I would say When we learned ben Beivai was in charge of the pakia, I would say wicks, as we learned They would tear mafkia from the priests’ worn-out trousers and belts and with which they lit once I heard that which is taught And moreover, lashes the person in charge of the pakia, I say: What a pakia? lashes.

An incident both of were equal running and ascending on the ramp, The Sages taught An incident two priests who were equal running and ascending the ramp. One of them reached the four cubits before his colleague, took a knife and stabbed him in the heart.

Rabbi Tzadok stood up on the steps of the Entrance Hall and said: Hear my brothers of the house of Israel. states: “If one be found slain in the land... then your Elders and your judges shall come forth us, Upon whom to bring the heifer whose neck is broken? on the city, or upon the courtyards, the entire people burst into tears.

The father of the boy, came and found that he was convulsing. He said: May be an atonement for you. my son is still convulsing the knife, has not become ritually impure to teach you that the ritual purity of utensils of more concern to them than the shedding of blood. And similarly, it says: “Furthermore, Manasseh spilled innocent blood very much, till he had filled Jerusalem from one end to another”
Which incident came first, If we say bloodshed Now, bloodshed they did not establish a lottery leg being broken they did establish Rather, leg was broken came first,

once they established the lottery, what were they doing the four cubits Rather, actually, that bloodshed came first. Initially, thought that it was merely a random, event, once they saw that in any event were coming to danger, the Sages established a lottery.

Rabbi Tzadok stood up on the steps of the Entrance Hall and said: Hear my brothers of the house of Israel. states: “If one be found slain in the land, us, Upon whom to bring upon the city, or upon the courtyards? Jerusalem subject to bringing a heifer whose neck is broken? Wasn’t it taught Ten things were said Jerusalem and this is one of them:
